marketState: neutral - bullish weather catalyst vs bearish storage surplus
summary: Front price ($2.911) sits almost exactly at fair value out to 14 days ($2.923-$2.985 fair, FAIR status) and only slightly under fair by +21D ($3.080, SLIGHTLY UNDER), so the market has not yet fully priced the sharp escalation in cooling demand: 1-7D demand is +9.6% vs 5y and 8-16D demand is +34.9% vs 5y with rangePos 1.23 (above the historical max), and rising fast (+11.5 in 24h). Managed money is net short 105,709 contracts at just the 8th percentile of the last 5 years - an unusually stretched short position that raises squeeze risk if the heat materializes. Working against that: storage is +180 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, spot is running -7.7pp (-14.6% YTD vs -6.9% median) below its seasonal median, and seasonality reliability is low (59pp 10-90% spread) with only 38% historical odds of higher prices 3-4 weeks out. Daily and weekly technicals remain bearish (EMA50<200, RSI mid-30s, negative MACD) even as intraday timeframes turn neutral-to-bullish, and the futures curve shows mild Aug/Sep backwardation but steep contango building into winter (Jan27 $4.16).

longTerm.rationale: Extreme 8-16D heat demand and an 8th-percentile managed-money short position both argue for higher prices, but a +180 Bcf storage surplus with a bearish build surprise, price already -7.7pp below seasonal median, and low-reliability seasonality (only 38% odds of higher prices 3-4 weeks out) make this too conflicted for a full-size multi-week position.
longTerm.invalidation: A break of the daily/weekly EMA50<200 bearish structure or storage deficit narrowing would flip this bullish; a further storage surplus expansion or a fading heat forecast would confirm bearish.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback into the $2.55-$2.70 zone (lower fair-value support) for a seasonal long, or confirmation via the next storage report that the 5y surplus is narrowing / MM shorts are covering.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D demand is +9.6% vs 5y and accelerating (+11.5 in 24h) while price is still FAIR vs fair value ($2.911 vs $2.923) leaving room to run, and the front carries mild backwardation to Sep (-$0.035) consistent with near-term tightness; the extreme, above-max 8-16D heat also raises short-squeeze odds given MM shorts at the 8th percentile.
shortTerm.invalidation: Daily/weekly bearish technical trend (RSI<40, MACD negative, EMA50<200) is the key counter-risk - a close back below $2.78 or a materially cooler forecast update invalidates the setup.

Managed money net short at 8th percentile of 5y range - squeeze risk
Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with bearish +22 Bcf build surprise

risks: Low seasonality reliability (59pp 10-90% spread) makes historical patterns a weak guide right now
